codeReview.summary: I reviewed the feature metadata, handoff, transcript skeleton, `factory-flow-worker` guidance, commit `3a8db8096d285146c288a31da3737f151e91b0bc`, and the current state of the touched RBAC/dispatch files. The route ownership and denial-copy changes are correct: `PortalRoleActionMatrix` now gates `POST /api/v1/sales/dispatch/confirm` to sales/admin, `DispatchController` stays on read-only `/api/v1/dispatch/**`, and the updated integration tests and frontend library notes consistently describe the sales-owned posting path. However, the feature does not fully satisfy its "align default role permissions" requirement for upgraded tenants. `SystemRole` removes `dispatch.confirm` from accounting/factory defaults, but the runtime synchronizers only add missing permissions and never prune retired ones from existing system-role rows, so legacy `ROLE_ACCOUNTING`/`ROLE_FACTORY` records keep stale `dispatch.confirm` grants after startup synchronization. I also found one non-blocking stale Javadoc comment in `DispatchController`.

issues.description: This feature moved `dispatch.confirm` from `ROLE_ACCOUNTING`/`ROLE_FACTORY` to `ROLE_SALES` in `SystemRole`, but both `synchronizeSystemRole(...)` and `synchronizeSystemRolePermissions(...)` are additive-only: they append missing default permissions and never remove retired ones. Because startup calls `RbacSynchronizationConfig.onApplicationEvent -> synchronizeSystemRoles()`, existing tenant roles keep legacy `dispatch.confirm` grants instead of being realigned. The gap is codified in `erp-domain/src/test/java/com/bigbrightpaints/erp/modules/rbac/service/RoleServiceTest.java:96-121`, which still treats `ROLE_ACCOUNTING` plus `dispatch.confirm` as already aligned. That means the feature does not actually align default role permissions end-to-end for upgraded environments.

The class Javadoc still says this controller is used by factory to confirm shipped quantities before dispatch, but `/api/v1/dispatch/**` is now read-only and final dispatch posting belongs to `SalesController.confirmDispatch`. The runtime behavior is correct; the comment is just stale and misleading.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The shared `factory-flow-worker` procedure does not explicitly remind workers to audit startup/backfill synchronization when a feature moves default permissions between system roles. This feature updated `SystemRole` and contract tests, but missed the additive-only RBAC synchronizers that preserve stale permissions on existing roles.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: `.factory/skills/factory-flow-worker/SKILL.md` focuses on controller/service/test/contract surfaces and validation commands, while this review found `erp-domain/src/main/java/com/bigbrightpaints/erp/modules/rbac/service/RoleService.java:201-257` still only adds permissions after `erp-domain/src/main/java/com/bigbrightpaints/erp/modules/rbac/domain/SystemRole.java:38-49` changed the default dispatch ownership.

summary: Fail: the sales-owned dispatch-confirm route/denial/docs changes look correct, but the feature leaves startup RBAC synchronization additive-only, so existing accounting/factory roles retain stale `dispatch.confirm` grants and default role permissions are not fully realigned.
